title: Shinjis EoE Morality refuse to fight masturbation
display_title: Shinjis EoE Morality refuse to fight masturbation
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's EoE Morality (refuse to fight / masturbation)             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Sun Jul 06, 2014 7:58 am One of the most controversial aspect of EoE is Shinji's refusal to save Asuka. It's his lowest point, him masturbating over Asuka is nothing by comparison. Many accept that the reason for his refusal to fight is simply depression caused by Kaworu's death -and other previous incidents; as depression lowers one's will to do anything. Others simply say it's because of his cowardice. But I won't accept that, because it seems to me that Shinji's refusal to fight in the past was never due to cowardice, at least not cowardice in the face of death. There's another aspect to the situation that doesn't seem to be considered. Here, the enemy are other humans. We know from ep. 18 and 24 what effect hurting/killing other people has on him. Imagine now a whole bunch of people that may be killed in the process. Could you imagine Shinji ruthlessly fighting like Asuka? For Asuka, conflict is a chance to win and prove one-self, for Shinji not so much. Even after Asuka went to fight the EVA series, Shinji didn't knew that they lacked real human pilots, while for Asuka this didn't even mattered. Let's take Shinji's own confession in front of Misato for example. It's really confusing, partially because the thoughts of someone in the middle of severe depression aren't the most lucid thoughts possible. Nevertheless, the first thing he says before taking it all on himself, is " I can't pilot and Eva if all I do is hurt and kill other people. " So this seems to be the real reason, the self-loathing probably comes from the disappointment with himself that ensues from this. As for why Anno didn't insist more on this point. For once, the thoughts of someone in the maelstrom of depression aren't the most rational ones -I'm talking about Shinji here, not Anno. Secondly, because EoE is about depression and not about pacifism. Also, this is not something felt only by Shinji himself. Maya, who's quite similar to Shinji in terms of emotionally responding to situations, refuses to fight for the same reasons. When asking later one of her colleagues who did fought if he thinks he's done the right thing, his response is that he can't possible know this. Indeed there's also the fact that Shinji couldn'tt know if him fighting would led to positive consequences or not. In the series it was simpler, Angels attacked and humans fought back. Even then, Shinji wondered why are things that way, what's the main reason behind the conflict. For other people it was simply the case of wild beast attacking a human settlement. Now tough, official military forces are the one who attack. The possibility of NERV having criminal intentions and JSDF punishing them was not to be dismissed, and Shinji already made his mind about how unscrupulous of a man Gendo is. From Shinji's point of view, it would have meant to kill other people who were no different than him in their justification, all in the name of his bastard father. Yeah, Shinji needed to fight either way, because the way JSDF acted didn't left room for any other kind of response, but Shnji's morale was very low and his pessimism was high. It seems to me that the incidents that affected Shinji before the movie started aren't enough to explain why he didn't acted in the first half of the movie. More likely, it all most be interpreted in the context of the movie at hand. As such, the reason was the human conflict itself in which Shinji didn't wanted to take part of of. The unfortunate incidents from ep. 23 and 24 and the depression that followed only lowered Shinji's morale enough to not see to fight as his duty and only see the negative aspects of his possible involvement. I think that Shinji would have wanted to see the fight as his duty and nothing more and wouldn't have backed of as such, but the situation had too many shades of grey and depression only made it even more grey. It was just a situation lacking a way out it seemed. So, I think that seen in this light, his passivity is easier to understand. It's both more character consistent and it's less questionable from a moral stand-point. What do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by EvaBrothers » Sun Jul 06, 2014 8:07 am I always saw Shinji as someone who not only longs for human contact, but who values human life deeply, too. He fought against Angels because they were a threat to humankind - but to protect humankind from humankind is a wholly different story. He simply can't decide over the righteousness of killing some human beings to protect other human beings. He might have tried to stop them maybe, but not kill them. But the psychological conditions he was in made it tough to even just try to stop them, I think.

[]: Re: Shinji's refusal to fight and the human factor in EoE             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 06, 2014 9:08 am HiImAnAlien wrote: One of the most controversial aspect of EoE is Shinji's refusal to save Asuka. It's his lowest point, him masturbating over Asuka is nothing by comparison.

[]: Re: Shinji's refusal to fight and the human factor in EoE             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 06, 2014 2:05 pm Bagheera wrote: He didn't refuse to fight for her, he was unable to ... bakelite

[]: Re: Shinji's refusal to fight and the human factor in EoE             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Sun Jul 06, 2014 2:15 pm Bagheera wrote: The hell it isn't. He didn't refuse to fight for her, he was unable to -- Eva in bakelite and all of that. His act in the hospital room, by contrast, was a deliberate act, one that both violated and objectified her. That pissed her off, and that's why she was yelling at him about it in P3II and didn't say a word about the MPE battle.

[]: Re: Shinji's refusal to fight and the human factor in EoE             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 06, 2014 2:30 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Only because he was attempting suicide by JSSDF rather than getting to his assigned station when the alert was sounded -- you know, in that interval where they were able to evacuate Asuka from her hospital room, stuff her in an entry plug and launch EVA-02 into the lake.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Jul 06, 2014 2:58 pm Shinji has a definite internal conflict over the act of killing, even if he's killing giant laser bugs. Hence nearly killing another pilot and killing the fake human Kaworu nearly breaking and then breaking him, respectively. He doesn't have an articulated moral stance of philosophy on the morality of piloting the Eva and killing. It's more of a gut instinct that he has to grapple with. He's a child soldier, after all, and a major theme of the series is destruction of his innocence.

[]:             Quote Post by HiImAnAlien » Sun Jul 06, 2014 3:44 pm Bagheera wrote: Masturbating over Asuka's unconscious body is sexual abuse, by definition.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 06, 2014 3:48 pm HiImAnAlien wrote: You seem to cling to some things and not let them go: that Shinji's wanking is the most evil, evil thing in the history of things being evil; that it was a reason for his depression rather than a consequence of it and that because Shinji is depressed and Shinji is Shinji, he can't think for shit.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Jul 06, 2014 4:49 pm Shinji's wanking is the most evil, evil thing in the history of things being evil; that it was a reason for his depression rather than a consequence of it, and that because Shinji is depressed and Shinji is Shinji, he can't think for shit.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:15 pm Sorrow wrote: I've read quite a few posts, old and new, over the past few days on this forum and you are the first person I've read to not think this is such an incredibly evil thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:32 pm pwhodges wrote: Actually, I don't think that either. But I've never entered discussions on this specific subject here for two reasons: (1) I have no idea how it is viewed in Japan, which is the target audience for the show; and (2) a rather high proportion of the forum here is American, and I have the impression that they tend* to express a more repressed view of sexual matters than I (a secular Briton) would - and I'd rather not get into any kind of argument over it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:35 pm I agree that it is what happened to Kaworu at his hands and to Toji, Rei and Asuka when he DIDN'T act that have him so overwhelemed. The wanking didn't help, obviously, but the problem was there before that. What I take issue with, however, is that because he didn't even try (regardless of if it would would have made a difference, which he can't have known) to help Asuka or to resist Seele in any way he essentially shit on the future that Kaworu, Rei2, and finally Misato died specifically to give him, and then in pre-instrumentality has the sheer gall to ask Asuka for help when he offered none? Seriously, fuck that guy.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:43 pm NemZ wrote: I agree that it is what happened to Kaworu at his hands and to Toji, Rei and Asuka when he DIDN'T act that have him so overwhelemed. The wanking didn't help, obviously, but the problem was there before that.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:43 pm Bagheera wrote: Is that really a matter of repression, though? I mean, it's kind of different from the typical "sex is bad" conservatism that's typical in the States.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:45 pm Bagheera wrote: Is that really a matter of repression, though? I mean, it's kind of different from the typical "sex is bad" conservatism that's typical in the States.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:46 pm pwhodges wrote: Well perhaps repression wasn't the best way to put it - but they seem pretty related to me anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:49 pm Considering how many people here want Shinji and Asuka to have sex I don't see how anyone would think that condemning Shinji for what he did comes from a "sex is bad" mindset. Even if she woke up, pulled him into the bed and they went to town, he'd still be guilty of a nonconsensual sexual act up until that point. Ironically, if she woke up before the special sauce was dispensed, grabbed Lil' Shinji and said "hey yo, lemme finish that for you" he'd slap her hand away and run off, which is what actually angered her about the whole thing in the first place. Also, I rarely do this, but I take exception to the whole "secular Briton" thing. Americans aren't all gun toting young earth creationists and I doubt anyone here fits that description. For most of us, the censorship and repression we experience is forced on us but a vocal but entrenched minority. Chuckman: Yes! Well said.

[]:             Quote Post by Sorrow » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:50 pm Yes, I know this is a generalisation bordering on stereotyping - but it is, none the less, my impression.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Jul 06, 2014 5:50 pm NemZ wrote: What I take issue with, however, is that because he didn't even try (regardless of if it would would have made a difference, which he can't have known) to help Asuka or to resist Seele in any way he essentially shit on the future that Kaworu, Rei2, and finally Misato died specifically to give him, and then in pre-instrumentality has the sheer gall to ask Asuka for help when he offered none? Seriously, fuck that guy.

Seriously, fuck that guy.
